creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Function calls, map lookups, and list indexing cannot be immediately called. The parser does not support chained call syntax.

**Repro:**
```
fn make_adder(n):
    return fn(x) x + n

make_adder(5)(3)          // Parse error - cannot call return value

actions = {"greet": fn(name) "Hello {name}"}
actions["greet"]("Alice") // Parse error - cannot call map lookup result

ops = [fn(a, b) a + b]
ops[0](1, 2)              // Parse error - cannot call list index result
```

**Workaround:** Assign to a temporary variable first.

This blocks natural functional programming patterns and is especially painful combined with the broken escaping closures.

**Test files:** `bugs/functions/t17b_immediate_call.rad`, `t29_fn_in_map.rad`, `t41_fn_list_call.rad`
title: Cannot immediately call result of an expression
